hero.name: João Guimarães
hero.card_bio: Fullstack Developer · Websites & Web Apps · Available for Freelance
hero.tagline: Passionate about creating intuitive and engaging user experiences. I specialize in transforming ideas into beautifully crafted products.

projects.title: Project One
projects.description: A full-stack web application built with Next.js and TypeScript. Focused on performance and developer experience.
projects.long_description:
This project was built to solve the challenge of delivering fast, accessible web experiences at scale. The architecture leverages Next.js App Router for server-side rendering, TypeScript for type safety across the entire codebase, and Tailwind CSS for a consistent design system.

Key technical decisions included implementing incremental static regeneration for dynamic content, setting up a CI/CD pipeline with automated testing, and optimizing Core Web Vitals to achieve a perfect Lighthouse score.

projects.title: Cascais Volleyball Cup
projects.description: The largest international volleyball tournament in southern Portugal. A full-featured event platform with team registration, live displays, photo gallery, and multi-language support.
projects.long_description:
Cascais Volleyball Cup is the largest international volleyball tournament in southern Portugal, bringing athletes and teams from across Europe to the Atlantic coast. The platform was built to handle every aspect of the event — from team sign-ups to match displays and media.

Key features include a multi-step team registration system with form validation, live schedule and score displays, an interactive photo gallery, and full internationalization supporting both Portuguese and English audiences.

projects.title: AR Field Service Platform
projects.description: A Unity 3D mobile AR app integrated with Microsoft Dynamics 365, Field Service, Teams, and Remote Assist — powered by TensorFlow object detection in C# and ML.NET.
projects.long_description:
This project combines two deeply integrated systems built to modernize field service operations. The Augmented Reality component is a Unity 3D mobile app running on Android that connects to Microsoft Dynamics 365, Field Service, Microsoft Teams, and Remote Assist — enabling technicians to overlay work orders, asset data, and remote guidance directly onto physical equipment through their devices.

Powering the recognition layer is a C# application built with ML.NET and TensorFlowSharp that performs real-time object detection on live camera feeds. Identified equipment is automatically matched to service records in Dynamics 365, bridging the physical and digital worlds in a seamless field workflow.

items.description: Building websites, web applications, and custom software for clients across different industries. Full-stack delivery using React / Next.js, .NET / Node.js, and Azure — from scoping and design to deployment and maintenance. Delivered the Cascais Volleyball Cup platform, a fully functioning major sport event site still active in Portugal.

Software development consultant embedded in client projects across enterprise environments. Built and maintained full-stack applications using .NET, C#, React, and Azure. Worked on production chatbots, Machine Learning DevOps architecture, and productivity workflows for enterprise clients across the full delivery lifecycle.

Head coach for two youth teams (Infantis A and Cadetes A) competing in the national league. Plan and run 4–5 training sessions per week, designing structured programs for player development. Manage competition preparation, in-game decisions, and post-match reviews.

about.bio:
I build websites, web apps, and custom software for businesses that need things done well.

Fullstack background across the Microsoft stack (.NET, Azure, Power Platform) and modern frontend (React, Next.js, Node.js) — from small business sites to production-grade applications. I've worked in consulting and freelance, taking projects from spec to deployment.

I can build websites & landing pages, web apps & internal tools, backend APIs & integrations, and custom automations and business workflows.

I work directly with clients — no layers, no overhead. You get a developer who owns the whole thing.
